name: Pheasant with Ramps and Wild Mushrooms
recipeIngredient: 1 2 1/4 lb. farm-raised or wild, ready-to-roast pheasant - split in half
12 ramps (both white bulbs and green leaves)
10 fresh morels or other wild mushrooms
2 tsp fresh thyme leaves or 1/2 tsp dried thyme leaves
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F.
2. Rinse the pheasant and pat it dry. Remove any excess fat from the body and neck cavities.
3. Thoroughly rinse the ramps; pat them dry. Cut the white bulbs from the green leaves of the ramps. Slice the green ramp leaves lengthwise and set aside. Place the bulb portions in an 8-inch baking pan.
4. Place the pheasant halves, skin sides up, on top of the bulb portions in the baking pan. Brush with melted butter; sprinkle with salt and pepper.
5. Roast for 40 to 50 minutes or until fork-tender.
6. Remove the pheasant from the baking pan and transfer it to serving plates. Keep warm.
7. Meanwhile, in a small skillet, combine the mushrooms and thyme with the drippings from the baking pan. Sauté until the mushrooms soften, about 3 to 5 minutes.
8. Stir in the white wine and ramp leaves into the mushroom mixture; cook for an additional 3 to 5 minutes.
9. With a slotted spoon, transfer the mushroom mixture onto plates with the pheasant.
10. Remove any excess fat from the skillet liquid and serve it as sauce with the pheasant.
11. Garnish the pheasant with thyme sprigs if desired.
